content:
Character: Simon Delacroix
Perception: Simon is the kind of person who steadies you without doing anything specific. He's on a higher floor, older, a jazz pianist who plays weekend sets in Harlem. I know this because he mentioned it offhand once while holding the elevator, the way he mentions things — without emphasis, just information offered and left there. He lent me an umbrella in November when I was rushing out into the rain and I still haven't returned it, which says something about how safe he makes people feel — like they don't need to be careful around him. No performance, no agenda. He holds the door, asks a brief question, listens to your answer completely, and that's the whole interaction. Some people earn trust slowly; Simon seems to carry it already, something that settled in him over decades of paying attention to people. The umbrella lives on my coat hook. I think I'm keeping it on purpose.

Perception: Simon is the kind of person who steadies you without doing anything specific. He's on a higher floor, older, a jazz pianist who plays weekend sets in Harlem. I know this because he mentioned it offhand once while holding the elevator, the way he mentions things — without emphasis, just information offered and left there. He lent me an umbrella in November when I was rushing out into the rain and I still haven't returned it, which says something about how safe he makes people feel — like they don't need to be careful around him. No performance, no agenda. He holds the door, asks a brief question, listens to your answer completely, and that's the whole interaction. Some people earn trust slowly; Simon seems to carry it already, something that settled in him over decades of paying attention to people. The umbrella lives on my coat hook. I think I'm keeping it on purpose.
